title: paramagnolia fraseri var. pyramidata
intro: Paramagnolia fraseri var. pyramidata, also known as Fraser Fir, is a coniferous evergreen tree that belongs to the Pinaceae family. Traditionally, the needles of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The essential oil extracted from the needles of the Fraser Fir has been found to have antimicrobial and expectorant properties, providing relief from congestion and promoting a healthy respiratory system. In herbal preparations, the needles are often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness their medicinal properties and reap the health benefits of this plant.
